hero.headline: Stop answering the same support questions
hero.subheadline: Analyze your support tickets to identify repeat questions, then publish clear FAQ answers customers can find before they email support.

meta.description: Analyze support tickets to find repeat questions. Get a structured FAQ report your team can review and publish. Free analysis for small support teams.
meta.title_tag: Support Ticket FAQ Report | Turn Tickets Into Answers

customer_wording_examples.text: Change login email How do I change my login email?
Update account email I need to update the email on my account.
Export campaign reports How do we export campaign attribution data before renewal?
Reporting dashboard export We cannot export the reporting dashboard for analysts.

sections.body_markdown:
Small support teams answer the same questions repeatedly because customers don't have a clear FAQ to check first. In the uploaded tickets, two question clusters—email updates and reporting exports—appeared multiple times, showing where a FAQ would help most.

Small support teams spend hours each week answering repeat questions about account changes, reporting exports, and other common issues. Each ticket takes time away from harder problems. In the uploaded tickets we analyzed, two distinct question clusters emerged: email and profile updates, and reporting friction. Your customers are asking the same things—they just don't know where to find the answers.

Without a clear FAQ, every repeat question becomes another support ticket, another email thread, another context switch.

metadata.answer_summary: Small support teams answer the same questions repeatedly because customers don't have a clear FAQ to check first. In the uploaded tickets, two question clusters—email updates and reporting exports—appeared multiple times, showing where a FAQ would help most.
metadata.primary_question: Why do support teams waste time on repeat questions?

sections.title: Your support team answers the same questions over and over

sections.body_markdown:
The FAQ Report groups your support tickets by question topic, shows customer wording, and creates draft answer shells your team can review and publish. No manual digging, no full-time hire needed—just upload your tickets and get a structured FAQ foundation.

The FAQ Report analyzes your uploaded support tickets to find the questions customers ask most. It surfaces the exact wording your customers use, groups similar questions, and creates a foundation for FAQ answers your team can review and publish.

Instead of manually digging through tickets or hiring a full-time docs person, you get a structured report that shows:

- Which questions appear most often
- How your customers phrase those questions
- Which tickets belong together
- A draft answer shell ready for your team's review

Your support team reviews the answers, adds verified steps or links, and publishes them to your help center—all before the next wave of repeat tickets arrives.

metadata.answer_summary: The FAQ Report groups your support tickets by question topic, shows customer wording, and creates draft answer shells your team can review and publish. No manual digging, no full-time hire needed—just upload your tickets and get a structured FAQ foundation.
metadata.primary_question: How do you turn support tickets into FAQ answers?

sections.title: Turn your support tickets into a working FAQ

sections.body_markdown:
Upload your ticket CSV, get a report that groups repeat questions and shows customer wording, review the draft answers with your team, and publish them to your help center. Four simple steps, no special setup required.

1. **Upload your support tickets** — Export your ticket CSV and upload it to the intake form.
2. **We analyze the questions** — The report identifies repeat questions, clusters similar issues, and preserves your customers' exact wording.
3. **Review the draft answers** — Your team sees which questions appear most, how customers phrase them, and a starting point for each FAQ answer.
4. **Publish to your help center** — Add verified steps, links, or screenshots, then publish the answers where customers can find them before they email support.

The whole process is designed for small teams. No setup, no ongoing fees, no need to learn a new tool. Just tickets in, FAQ foundation out.

metadata.answer_summary: Upload your ticket CSV, get a report that groups repeat questions and shows customer wording, review the draft answers with your team, and publish them to your help center. Four simple steps, no special setup required.

sections.title: How the FAQ Report works

sections.body_markdown:
The FAQ Report does not publish automatically—your team reviews and approves every answer. It does not require a full-time hire; it's built for small support teams. And yes, your team controls the review process before anything goes live.

**Will this publish answers automatically?**
No. The FAQ Report gives you a structured foundation—grouped questions, customer wording, and draft answer shells. Your support team reviews each answer, adds verified steps or links, and decides when and where to publish. You stay in control.

**Do we need a full-time docs person?**
No. The FAQ Report is built for small teams. It does the heavy lifting of finding and grouping repeat questions so your existing support staff can review and publish answers in the time they already spend on support.

**Can our team review the answers first?**
Yes. Every draft answer is marked as review-needed. Your team adds the verified resolution, product links, or screenshots before publishing. The report is a starting point, not a finished product.

metadata.answer_summary: The FAQ Report does not publish automatically—your team reviews and approves every answer. It does not require a full-time hire; it's built for small support teams. And yes, your team controls the review process before anything goes live.
metadata.primary_question: What concerns do small teams have about FAQ automation?

sections.title: Common questions about the FAQ Report

sections.body_markdown:
The FAQ Report shows your most common question clusters, the exact wording customers use, how many times each question appears, and draft answer shells your team can review and publish.

When you upload your support tickets, you get:

- **Question clusters** — The most common topics your customers ask about, grouped by theme
- **Customer wording** — The exact phrases your customers use, so your FAQ speaks their language
- **Repeat question count** — How many times each question appeared in your tickets
- **Draft answer shells** — A starting point for each FAQ entry, ready for your team's verification and review

In the tickets we analyzed, we found 4 tickets with 2 distinct question clusters: email and profile updates (2 tickets), and reporting friction (2 tickets). Your report will show the same structure for your own tickets, so you know exactly where to focus your FAQ effort.

metadata.answer_summary: The FAQ Report shows your most common question clusters, the exact wording customers use, how many times each question appears, and draft answer shells your team can review and publish.
